underlying_goal: Install Ubuntu system updates on a Hetzner server and configure passwordless sudo for apt, then install Google Antigravity CLI, then explore Claude optimization plugins
friction_detail: Claude repeatedly suggested manual terminal steps and long one-liners that wrapped/broke on paste, and incorrectly claimed not to know the user's password when -S was suggested.
brief_summary: User wanted apt updates + passwordless sudo setup + Antigravity install; updates ran and Antigravity was installed, but sudoers config failed repeatedly due to terminal/paste issues and Claude's suboptimal suggestions.
